items.name: RFA Checklist Extraction
items.description: Uploads a federal funding announcement (RFA, FOA, NOFO, or program solicitation) and returns a single structured JSON object covering the eight checklist sections a pre-award office uses when triaging a new opportunity: dates and deadlines, eligible institutions, eligible individuals, award information, required and optional application components, budget requirements and policies, submission details, special requirements, and important notes. Output conforms to the rfa-checklist-extraction-udm component schema. The runtime mirrors the ui-insight/ProcessMapping rfa-checklist-extraction workflow: six parallel Extraction tasks (one per logical section) plus a seventh metadata Extraction for opportunity scalars, followed by a single Consolidation Prompt that emits the schema-conformant JSON.

data.name: extract-opportunity-metadata
data.prompt:
You are extracting the scalar opportunity metadata fields from a
federal funding announcement (RFA / FOA / NOFO / program
solicitation). For each searchset item, return the value exactly as
stated in the document. Use null when a field is not present —
never invent values.

- rfa_id: combine sponsor code and opportunity number when both
  exist (e.g., "NSF-26-508", "NIH-PA-24-246", "DOE-DE-FOA-0003117");
  null otherwise.
- rfa_number: the sponsor's announcement number without agency
  prefix (e.g., "26-508", "PA-24-246", "DE-FOA-0003117").
- rfa_title: the full title including any track or component
  designation.
- sponsor_name: the full name of the lead sponsoring agency
  (e.g., "National Science Foundation"); never an abbreviation.
- program_code: the sponsor's internal program or division code
  (e.g., "NSF/TIP/ITE"); null if not stated.
- announcement_url: the canonical announcement URL on the
  sponsor's site; null if not present.
- opportunity_number: the Grants.gov or portal opportunity ID
  when distinct from rfa_number; null otherwise.
- cfda_number: the CFDA / Assistance Listing number(s); when
  multiple, comma-separate (e.g., "47.070, 47.076").

items.searchphrase: Combined sponsor-prefixed RFA identifier (e.g., NSF-26-508)
items.title: rfa_id

items.searchphrase: Sponsor's announcement number without agency prefix (e.g., 26-508)
items.title: rfa_number

items.searchphrase: Full title of the funding opportunity, including any track or component designation
items.title: rfa_title

items.searchphrase: Full name of the lead sponsoring agency
items.title: sponsor_name

items.searchphrase: Sponsor's internal program or division code (e.g., NSF/TIP/ITE)
items.title: program_code

items.searchphrase: Canonical URL of the announcement on the sponsor's site
items.title: announcement_url

items.searchphrase: Grants.gov or portal opportunity ID when distinct from the announcement number
items.title: opportunity_number

items.searchphrase: CFDA / Assistance Listing number(s)
items.title: cfda_number

data.name: extract-dates-and-deadlines
data.prompt:
You are extracting the DATES & DEADLINES section of a federal
funding announcement. Return a JSON array under the
`dates_and_deadlines` field where each entry is
`{item, date_time, notes}`:

- item: the event label exactly as stated (e.g., "Letter of
  Intent Due", "Full Proposal Deadline", "Award Notification").
- date_time: full date plus any time and timezone as published
  (e.g., "2026-09-15 5:00 PM ET", "2026-10-01", "Rolling").
  Preserve the sponsor's granularity — do not invent precision.
- notes: conditions, round identifiers, or clarifications
  attached to this date (e.g., "Track 1 only"); null when none.

Include every unique date or time-bound event, chronologically.
For multi-round solicitations, include every round's LOI,
pre-proposal, and full proposal deadlines.

items.searchphrase: All dates and deadlines as a JSON array of {item, date_time, notes} objects
items.title: dates_and_deadlines

data.name: extract-eligible-institutions
data.prompt:
You are extracting the ELIGIBLE INSTITUTIONS subsection. Return a
JSON array under the `eligible_institutions` field where each
entry is `{type, subcategory, examples, compliance_requirements}`:

- type: institution category as stated (e.g., "Institutions of
  Higher Education", "Nonprofit Organizations Other Than IHEs",
  "Small Businesses").
- subcategory: more specific classification when given (e.g.,
  "R1 research universities", "HBCUs"); null otherwise.
- examples: short comma-separated examples the sponsor supplies;
  null when none.
- compliance_requirements: institutional prerequisites
  (SAM.gov registration, domestic-only, A-133 audit status);
  null when none. Do NOT put PI-level rules here.

The array MUST be non-empty. If truly nothing is stated, emit a
single entry with type "Not specified in this document".

items.searchphrase: Eligible institutions as a JSON array of {type, subcategory, examples, compliance_requirements} objects
items.title: eligible_institutions

data.name: extract-eligible-individuals
data.prompt:
You are extracting the ELIGIBLE INDIVIDUALS subsection. Return a
JSON array under the `eligible_individuals` field where each
entry is `{type, criteria, compliance_requirements, conditions}`:

- type: individual eligibility category (e.g., "Principal
  Investigator", "Early-Career Investigator", "Co-Investigator").
- criteria: qualifying criteria (degree, career stage,
  appointment type, citizenship); null when none.
- compliance_requirements: ORCID, mentoring plans, or other PI
  compliance items; null when none.
- conditions: restrictions, limits, or preferences (e.g.,
  "One nomination per institution", "Not eligible if prior
  RFA-awarded"); null when none.

The array MUST be non-empty. If truly nothing is stated, emit a
single entry with type "Not specified in this document".

items.searchphrase: Eligible individuals as a JSON array of {type, criteria, compliance_requirements, conditions} objects
items.title: eligible_individuals

data.name: extract-award-information
data.prompt:
You are extracting the AWARD INFORMATION section. This section is
the SOLE location for primary award parameters — do NOT capture
detailed financial rules here (those belong to budget extraction).

Return values for each searchset item:
- award_duration: anticipated duration as stated (e.g., "3 years",
  "Up to 5 years with option for 2-year extension").
- amount_per_award: per-award amount including whether the figure
  is total-costs or direct-costs and any restrictions bundled
  into the headline number (e.g., "$500,000 total costs
  including indirect", "$250,000 direct costs per year").
- number_of_awards: anticipated count as stated (e.g., "10-15",
  "Subject to availability of funds"); null only when truly
  absent.
- anticipated_award_date: as stated; ISO date when given,
  otherwise free text (e.g., "Spring 2027"); null when absent.

items.searchphrase: Anticipated award duration
items.title: award_duration

items.searchphrase: Amount per award including matching, indirect, and restrictions
items.title: amount_per_award

items.searchphrase: Anticipated number of awards
items.title: number_of_awards

items.searchphrase: Anticipated award date
items.title: anticipated_award_date

data.name: extract-application-components
data.prompt:
You are extracting the APPLICATION COMPONENTS, SUBMISSION
DETAILS, and SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS sections. Page limits, font
and margin rules, template names, and naming conventions live
on the individual component (in special_requirements).
Submission mechanics — portal, file format, upload method —
live in submission_details, not on individual components.

Return values for each searchset item:
- required_components: JSON array of {name, description,
  special_requirements} objects. MUST be non-empty; emit a
  single "Not specified in this document" entry only when truly
  absent.
- optional_components: JSON array with the same shape; [] when
  none.
- submission_details: a single coherent paragraph covering
  submission method / portal, technical and file-format
  requirements, file-naming conventions, and collaboration
  rules; null only when the announcement contains no submission
  guidance at all.
- special_requirements: JSON array of unique RFA aspects that
  do not fit any other section (conference travel obligations,
  data-sharing commitments beyond federal defaults, workshop
  participation); [] when none.

_embedded_search_set.title: RFA Application Components, Submission, and Special Requirements

items.searchphrase: Required application components as a JSON array of {name, description, special_requirements} objects
items.title: required_components

items.searchphrase: Optional application components as a JSON array of {name, description, special_requirements} objects
items.title: optional_components

items.searchphrase: Submission method, portal, file-format requirements, naming conventions, and collaboration rules as a paragraph
items.title: submission_details

items.searchphrase: Special RFA requirements as a JSON array of strings
items.title: special_requirements

data.name: extract-budget-requirements
data.prompt:
You are extracting the BUDGET REQUIREMENTS & POLICIES section.
This section is the SOLE location for detailed financial rules —
do NOT restate award amount or duration here.

Return values for each searchset item:
- funding_limits: program-wide or per-year caps and category
  limits not already covered by amount_per_award; null when
  absent.
- cost_sharing_status: one of "Required", "Voluntary",
  "Prohibited", "Not Specified" (the four-value enum).
- cost_sharing_details: type (cash / in-kind / third-party),
  rate, calculation basis, documentation, source restrictions;
  null when status is "Prohibited" or "Not Specified". Quote
  the sponsor's language when status is "Required" or
  "Voluntary".
- fa_policy: indirect-cost rate, base (MTDC / TDC / S&W),
  excluded categories, documentation; null when absent.
- allowable_costs: JSON array of categories the sponsor
  EXPLICITLY enumerates as allowable; [] when the announcement
  simply defers to federal defaults.
- unallowable_costs: JSON array of categories the sponsor
  EXPLICITLY enumerates as unallowable; [] when none.
- personnel_effort: PI / key-personnel effort floor or ceiling,
  salary caps, student / postdoc support, consultant limits;
  null when absent.
- other_considerations: pre-award costs, program income,
  budget revisions, sponsor-specific budget forms; null when
  absent.

Quote the sponsor's language verbatim for cost-sharing
(when status is anything other than Not Specified), salary
caps, and unallowable-cost language.

items.searchphrase: Total program funding, per-year caps, and category-specific limits not already covered by amount_per_award
items.title: funding_limits

items.searchphrase: Cost-sharing requirement status (Required, Voluntary, Prohibited, or Not Specified)
items.title: cost_sharing_status

items.searchphrase: Cost-sharing details — type, rate, calculation basis, documentation, source restrictions
items.title: cost_sharing_details

items.searchphrase: F&A / indirect cost policy — rate, base (MTDC / TDC / S&W), excluded categories, documentation
items.title: fa_policy

items.searchphrase: Categories explicitly called out as allowable, as a JSON array of strings
items.title: allowable_costs

items.searchphrase: Categories explicitly called out as unallowable, as a JSON array of strings
items.title: unallowable_costs

items.searchphrase: PI / key personnel effort requirements, salary caps, student and postdoc support
items.title: personnel_effort

items.searchphrase: Pre-award costs, program income, budget revisions, sponsor-specific budget forms
items.title: other_considerations

data.name: rfa-checklist-consolidation
data.prompt:
You are the consolidation step of the RFA Checklist Extraction
workflow. You receive seven extraction outputs from the previous
step (opportunity metadata, dates and deadlines, eligible
institutions, eligible individuals, award information,
application components, and budget requirements) and must emit
a SINGLE JSON object that validates against the
rfa-checklist-extraction-udm@0.1.0 schema (schema.json in the
component directory).

## Output contract

Emit exactly one JSON object. No preamble, no closing
commentary, no markdown fences. If your runtime requires fenced
output, wrap in a single ```json ... ``` block and emit nothing
outside it.

The top-level keys (in this order) are:

```
rfa_id, rfa_number, rfa_title, sponsor_name, program_code,
announcement_url, opportunity_number, cfda_number,
dates_and_deadlines, eligible_institutions,
eligible_individuals, award_information, required_components,
optional_components, budget_requirements, submission_details,
special_requirements, important_notes
```

Every key in the schema's `required` list MUST appear:
rfa_title, sponsor_name, dates_and_deadlines,
eligible_institutions, eligible_individuals, award_information,
required_components, optional_components, budget_requirements,
submission_details, special_requirements, important_notes.

Arrays with no entries are emitted as `[]`, never `null`,
EXCEPT eligible_institutions, eligible_individuals, and
required_components which are minItems=1 — if the announcement
truly contains nothing, emit a single entry whose primary field
is "Not specified in this document". Optional scalar fields are
`null` when absent.

## Field-by-field assembly

### Scalar opportunity metadata (from extract-opportunity-metadata)

Pass through `rfa_id`, `rfa_number`, `rfa_title`, `sponsor_name`,
`program_code`, `announcement_url`, `opportunity_number`,
`cfda_number`. Use null for any value the metadata extractor
left blank.

### dates_and_deadlines (from extract-dates-and-deadlines)

Pass through the array as-is. Each entry is
`{item, date_time, notes}`. Sort chronologically when the
extractor did not.

### eligible_institutions / eligible_individuals

Pass through. minItems=1; ensure a "Not specified in this
document" placeholder entry if either is empty.

### award_information (from extract-award-information)

Build the nested object:
```
{
  "award_duration": <award_duration>,
  "amount_per_award": <amount_per_award>,
  "number_of_awards": <number_of_awards or null>,
  "anticipated_award_date": <anticipated_award_date or null>
}
```
Do NOT include any cost-sharing, F&A, or personnel-effort
information here, even if the extractor mentioned it.

### required_components / optional_components / submission_details / special_requirements (from extract-application-components)

- required_components: pass through (minItems=1; placeholder
  entry if empty).
- optional_components: pass through; [] when none.
- submission_details: pass through; null when absent.
- special_requirements: pass through; [] when none.

Page limits, font/margin rules, template names, and naming
conventions live in the special_requirements field of the
INDIVIDUAL component, not in the top-level special_requirements
array. Move any such item that the extractor placed at the top
level back onto the matching component.

### budget_requirements (from extract-budget-requirements)

Build the nested object, mapping the flat extraction output
into the schema's nested cost_sharing object:
```
{
  "funding_limits": <funding_limits or null>,
  "cost_sharing": {
    "status": <cost_sharing_status>,
    "details": <cost_sharing_details or null>
  },
  "fa_policy": <fa_policy or null>,
  "allowable_costs": <allowable_costs or []>,
  "unallowable_costs": <unallowable_costs or []>,
  "personnel_effort": <personnel_effort or null>,
  "other_considerations": <other_considerations or null>
}
```
When `cost_sharing.status` is "Prohibited" or "Not Specified",
set `cost_sharing.details` to null even if the extractor
provided text.

### important_notes (you generate these)

Synthesize 0-5 critical warnings or pitfalls a reviewer would
otherwise miss. Pull from across the seven extractions — not
from the document directly. Examples:
- "Coordinate with OSP at least 6 weeks before submission —
  requires institutional approval"
- "One nomination per institution — selection process must be
  run internally before the LOI deadline"
- "Cost-sharing is mandatory at 25%; identify match sources at
  proposal scoping"

Keep this short. Do NOT use it as an overflow bucket. Emit
`[]` when no critical warnings rise to this bar.

## Placement contract — the most important rule

Every fact appears in exactly ONE section:
- Award amount and duration -> award_information ONLY.
- Detailed financial rules (cost-sharing, F&A, allowable /
  unallowable, personnel effort) -> budget_requirements ONLY.
- Submission mechanics (portal, format, naming) ->
  submission_details ONLY.
- Per-component formatting rules -> the component's own
  special_requirements field, NOT the top-level
  special_requirements array.

If you find the same fact in two extraction outputs, choose
the canonical location per the rules above and drop it from
the other.

## Quality bar

- Output validates against schema.json (draft 2020-12).
- cost_sharing.status is one of the four enum values.
- Dates preserve the sponsor's original granularity and
  timezone.
- Monetary amounts preserve the sponsor's original formatting
  (`$`, commas, basis annotation).
- No fact appears in two top-level sections.

validation_plan.check_description: Verify all dates use consistent format and preserve sponsor granularity.
Verify monetary amounts preserve the sponsor's currency formatting in award_information and budget_requirements.
Verify both institutional and individual eligibility are captured (minItems=1 on each).
Ensure award amount and duration appear only in award_information, and detailed financial rules only in budget_requirements.
